top_stories.title: Palo Alto Networks integrates CyberArk, Koi and Portkey and launches Idira to bolster AI and identity security
top_stories.summary: Palo Alto Networks completed the acquisition of CyberArk in February 2026 and is integrating CyberArk's privileged access management capabilities into its Cortex and Strata platforms. The integration focuses on strengthening identity verification for AI agents, alongside additional acquisitions of Koi and Portkey and the launch of a new product called Idira.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Self-Service Password Reset abused in Azure data theft attacks
top_stories.summary: A threat actor is abusing Microsoft's Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR) and other legitimate Azure/Microsoft 365 administration features to steal data from production environments. The attacks exploit built-in identity and access management functionality rather than traditional malware, targeting Microsoft 365 and Azure tenants.

top_stories.title: The New Phishing Click: How OAuth Consent Bypasses MFA
top_stories.summary: A phishing-as-a-service platform called EvilTokens compromised over 340 Microsoft 365 organizations across five countries within five weeks by abusing the OAuth device code flow to bypass MFA. The attack method directs victims to Microsoft's legitimate device login page, where completing the normal MFA challenge inadvertently grants attackers OAuth tokens, rendering MFA protections ineffective.

top_stories.title: How Storm-2949 turned a compromised identity into a cloud-wide breach
top_stories.summary: Storm-2949 executed a cloud-wide breach by leveraging compromised credentials to move laterally and exfiltrate data at scale without deploying malware. The attack demonstrates how stolen identity credentials alone can enable full cloud environment compromise by exploiting trusted authentication pathways.

top_stories.title: Critical Microsoft Vulnerabilities Doubled: From Exposure to Escalation
top_stories.summary: Microsoft critical vulnerabilities doubled year-over-year in 2025 while the total vulnerability count remained steady, according to BeyondTrust research. The report highlights a shift toward privilege escalation and identity abuse as primary attacker focus areas.

news.title: When your AI assistant has the keys to production
news.summary: An article examines the security risks of agentic AI systems that have autonomous access to production infrastructure, framing it as a confused-deputy problem. The analysis covers how LLMs operating in IT/network operations roles with execution privileges create authorization and access control vulnerabilities when they can be manipulated into misusing their delegated permissions.

news.title: What happens when your identity provider becomes the kill chain
news.summary: A Help Net Security video features Atsign CTO Colin Constable discussing how identity providers have become central attack targets in cyberattacks. The presentation covers how attackers exploit stolen session cookies, tokens, and OAuth consent grants, and how shared secrets between browser and server remain vulnerable even with TLS encryption due to intermediaries like CDNs and load balancers.

news.title: Verizon DBIR: Enterprises Face a Dangerous Vulnerability Glut
news.summary: Verizon's 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report found that exploits are now involved in 31% of initial access vectors in breaches. The report highlights that patching cadence is falling behind attacker exploitation speeds, contributing to enterprise vulnerability exposure.

news.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
news.summary: CISA accidentally exposed secrets and credentials in a GitHub repository that was publicly accessible since November 2025. The repository, ironically named "Private-CISA," contained sensitive credentials that should have been kept secure.

news.title: PureLogs infostealer is stealing credentials worldwide
news.summary: Fortinet researchers discovered a phishing campaign delivering the PureLogs information stealer via steganography, hiding encrypted malicious payloads inside cat photos. The malware targets Windows machines using invoice-themed lures and TXZ archives, with the primary objective of stealing credentials from victims worldwide.

news.title: Compromised Nx Console 18.95.0 Targeted VS Code Developers with Credential Stealer
news.summary: A compromised version of the Nx Console VS Code extension (version 18.95.0) was published to the Microsoft Visual Studio Code Marketplace, targeting developers with a credential stealer. The malicious extension, which has over 2.2 million installations, was designed to harvest credentials from developers using VS Code, Cursor, and JetBrains editors.

news.title: Popular GitHub Action Tags Redirected to Imposter Commit to Steal CI/CD Credentials
news.summary: Threat actors compromised the GitHub Actions workflow "actions-cool/issues-helper" by redirecting existing repository tags to a malicious imposter commit. The attack targets CI/CD pipeline credentials, harvesting and exfiltrating sensitive credentials to an attacker-controlled server.

news.title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
news.summary: Three separate supply chain attacks targeting npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub occurred within a 48-hour window, all focused on stealing secrets from developer environments and CI/CD pipelines. The stolen credentials included API keys, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and authentication tokens stored in developer workstations and pipeline configurations.

news.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, n8n Patch RCE, SQL Injection, Privilege Escalation Flaws
news.summary: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, and n8n have released security patches addressing vulnerabilities including authentication bypass and privilege escalation flaws. A critical flaw in Ivanti Xtraction (CVE-2026-8043, CVSS 9.6) is among the vulnerabilities patched, capable of enabling information disclosure or client-side attacks.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system details in a public GitHub repository until recently. The leaked repository contained files describing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes alongside the access credentials.

news.title: BeyondTrust Named an Overall Leader in 2026 KuppingerCole Analysts Leadership Compass for Privileged Access Management for Sixth Consecutive Year
news.summary: BeyondTrust has been named an Overall Leader in the 2026 KuppingerCole Leadership Compass for Privileged Access Management for the sixth consecutive year. The recognition highlights the company's capabilities in just-in-time and ephemeral access, AI agent governance, cross-domain privilege graph, session monitoring, and identity and DevOps integrations.

news.title: WinMagic Responds to New CISA OT Guidance With Transport-Layer Identity Architecture
news.summary: WinMagic has introduced a transport-layer identity architecture featuring Live Key and Live Identity technologies in response to new CISA operational technology (OT) security guidance. The company argues that existing Zero Trust models are inadequate for critical infrastructure environments where continuous uptime and persistent trust are required, proposing an endpoint-driven alternative approach.

news.title: GetReal Security Launches First Trust and Authenticity Platform That Combines Real-Time Continuous Identity Verification and Deepfake Detection
news.summary: GetReal Security has launched a platform called GetReal Protect that combines real-time continuous identity verification with deepfake detection across voice and video communications. The platform positions itself as the first solution to address both authentication and deepfake threats simultaneously, amid reported $1.6 billion in deepfake-related financial losses in 2025.

news.title: CISA Adds Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20182 to KEV After Admin Access Exploits
news.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-20182, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies are required to remediate the issue by May 17, 2026.

news.title: The Next Cybersecurity Challenge May Be Verifying AI Agents
news.summary: AI agents are emerging as a new challenge for cybersecurity professionals who must verify and manage their identities and access. The article covers the need for trusted identity standards, verification mechanisms, and runtime controls as AI agents increasingly operate autonomously within enterprise environments.

news.title: Palo Alto Networks Idira: A next-gen platform for human, agentic & machine identity security
news.summary: Palo Alto Networks has unveiled Idira, a trademarked next-generation identity security platform focused on human, agentic, and machine identity security. The platform is positioned around AI-era identity challenges, addressing security across multiple identity types.

news.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
news.summary: Cisco has released patches for a maximum-severity authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-20182, CVSS 10.0) in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and SD-WAN Manager that has been actively exploited. The flaw affects peering authentication mechanisms, allowing attackers to gain administrative access to affected systems.

news.title: Regional routing for AWS access portals: Implementing custom vanity domains for IAM Identity Center
news.summary: AWS has introduced regional routing capabilities for IAM Identity Center access portals, enabling custom vanity domains alongside the recently launched multi-Region replication feature. The update allows customers to replicate IAM Identity Center configurations across regions while implementing custom domain names for their workforce SSO access portals.

news.title: Identity breaches are on the rise, and are only getting worse - so how can your business stay safe?
news.summary: CyberArk has issued a warning about rising identity breaches, highlighting AI agents' access to sensitive files as an emerging threat vector. The company is advocating for a unified, automated identity security approach to address the increasing frequency and severity of identity-related incidents.

news.title: 10 best ITGC tools and software for automated IT controls in 2026
news.summary: The article covers IT General Controls (ITGC) tools and software for automated compliance and audit processes, including user access permission validation. Access controls and user access reviews are components of the broader topic, but the article is primarily focused on audit and compliance tooling rather than IAM directly.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a senior member of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft for his role in a series of SMS phishing attacks in 2022. The attacks compromised at least a dozen major technology companies through text-message phishing, enabling cryptocurrency theft from investors.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to silently compromise user sessions at scale.
